prevalence.trend: stable despite interventions [high burden persists due to dietary and socioeconomic factors]

riskFactors: Vegetarian diet low in heme iron
Socioeconomic factors limiting access to fortified foods
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: WHO criteria: Hb <12 g/dL women, <13 g/dL men, low serum ferritin

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Limited coverage via RMNCH+A program [~30-40% in targeted groups]
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 20% [limited success due to low compliance and implementation gaps]

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Free iron-folic acid (IFA) tablets via National Iron Plus Initiative, deworming, fortification programs
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenages: Highest global burden, poor dietary diversity, vegetarian diets, hookworm/malaria coinfections, low compliance with IFA
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National Iron Plus Initiative, Anemia Mukt Bharat program (2018), food fortification with iron
